signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.53; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) and retail 89% short (extreme retail short). HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive or retail capitulation would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral; GBP/USD at 1.3491 mid-range. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM −90,229. ATM IV 9.5% near HV; 25d RR flat — options neutral. Next resistance at 1.3600.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs retail 62% short — specs fading a potential RBNZ hike. NZD/USD at 0.5889; 0.5900 is the next resistance. Positioning and retail data imply a balanced view; a decisive move expected post-RBNZ meeting.
